name: Monaco

colors.symbolism: the gules of the Grimaldi arms (a fusily of silver and red)
the argent of the Grimaldi arms

description: Two equal horizontal stripes — red over white — in proportions 4:5.
history: Red and white have been the heraldic livery colours of the House of Grimaldi, the ruling family of Monaco, since the late 13th century; the Grimaldi arms are 'fusily argent and gules' (lozenges of silver and red). The simple horizontal red-over-white bicolour was officially adopted by Prince Charles III on 4 April 1881. The flag is essentially identical in design to that of Indonesia (which is taller, 2:3) and the historical flag of Poland was the inverse (white over red); Monaco's predates the Indonesian flag.

features.symbolism: the heraldic livery colours of the House of Grimaldi, the ruling family of Monaco since 1297
